ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: Yiti ɔhyele bɛ guale kuku nu wɔ angɔbeahene n’awuro. Né Yosef koso la sɔ kuku ne anu bie.
translated: The king's horse was tied under the mango tree. And Joseph also tied his sheep there.

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: Na ɔhane kyɛ, “Me mene mɔ, mesrɛ ɛmɔ ala, ɛmɔbra beesikye ɛmɔ akoaa awuro ɛnɛ kɔngɔen ye, ná ɛmɔnwunzi ɛmɔ gya bo; ná ngyerɛmɔ tutuutu ɛmɔadwɔso bahɔ b’atee.” Na bebuale kyɛ, “Daabi, yɛkɔla dwaaso.”
translated: The monkey said, "I and you, let's go, let's go to the farm, let's go harvest corn today and come back, and let's roast it and eat; and let's take the path through the big market." And the tortoise said, "No, let's go by the river."

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Yakob sili nu toale ye ateetulɛ ne aso, na Nyameɛ abɔfo mɔ yiale ye.
translated: Jacob was left alone, and a man wrestled with him until daybreak.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Na sɛ nnomaa bɔ bedi naen batena naen fuin nemɔ aso a, né Abram afoa bɛ.
translated: If birds do not sing, the hawk remains in its nest; so did Abram.

ref: Next, seven skinny, bony cows came up out of the river. I have never seen such terrible looking cows anywhere in Egypt.
text: Ye si ye, nandwie nzoo foforɛ bɔ bɛ nwo yɛ tɛ na bafɔ nyangenaen lili bɛ si wale. Nnwuni bɛ sɛso le wɔ Misraim aseɛ so nekaa biala.
translated: And it came to pass, that at midnight the LORD smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh that sat on his throne unto the firstborn of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the firstborn of cattle.
